name: Chocolate Caramel Pecan Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
CHOCOLATE CARAMEL FILLING:
24 ounces cream cheese
1/3 cup dark brown sugar -- packed
1/4 cup dark corn syrup
2 tablespoons cornstarch
3 large eggs
1 large egg yolk
1/3 cup sour cream
1 1/4 teaspoons vanilla extract
1 3/4 cups milk chocolate chips -- melted
1/3 cup chopped pecans
CHOCOLATE CARAMEL TOPPING:
3/4 cup milk chocolate chips
1/4 cup sour cream
1 tablespoon dark brown sugar
3 tablespoons chopped pecans
16 pecan halves
10 caramels -- indiv wrapped
2 tablespoons sour cream
CRUST:
In a small bowl, stir together crushed cookies and chopped pecans. Stir in melted butter until well combined. Press crumb mixture evenly onto the bottom of a greased 9-inch springform pan.
FILLING:
In a large bowl, combine cream cheese, brown sugar, corn syrup, and cornstarch. Beat with an electric mixer until smooth. Add eggs and egg yolk one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in sour cream and vanilla. Stir in melted chocolate chips and pecans. Pour cream cheese mixture over crust.
Bake at 350°F for 15 minutes. Lower the oven temperature to 200°F. Bake an additional 70 minutes or until center is no longer wet or shiny. Remove cake from the oven and run a knife around the inside edge of the pan. Turn off the oven; return the cake to the oven for an additional hour. Chill, uncovered, overnight.
TOPPING:
In a small saucepan, melt chocolate chips over low heat, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and stir in 1/4 cup sour cream and brown sugar. Stir in pecans. Spread warm sour cream mixture over cheesecake. Arrange pecan halves on top.
Melt caramels over boiling water in a double boiler. Stir in the 2 tablespoons sour cream. Drizzle caramel mixture over cheesecake. Chill until serving time.
